mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings and lower equity risk premiums over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136214.0 as of June 2012 shows continued employment growth
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with stable labor demand
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC with PAYEMS falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, the zero-lower-bound constraint signals extended monetary accommodation. This regime produces positive equity returns as investors reach for yield and discount rates remain compressed, driving S&P 500 appreciation over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.24% as of June 2012 indicates post-crisis accommodation still in effect
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence. This dual deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1588.0 and RSAFS at 369865.0 as of May 2012
regime_hint: late-cycle demand slowdown or early recession warning
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term (HOUST decline × RSAFS decline) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 2% annualized signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors interpret rising production as confirmation of economic momentum and profit growth.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 112.3227 as of May 2012 reflects post-crisis manufacturing recovery
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with improving capacity utilization
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth months produce negative 5-day returns in more than 58% of instances, or Rank IC with INDPRO falls below 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 4.0% signals slack in the labor market and subdued wage pressure. Under this regime, the Federal Reserve maintains accommodative policy, keeping real discount rates low and supporting equity valuations over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% as of June 2012 indicates persistent labor market slack
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with elevated unemployment and accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 52% of months when UNRATE > 4.0%, or the relationship between UNRATE level and subsequent returns reverses sign (becomes positive correlation) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.3%) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real asset values, producing negative 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 201.7 and FEDFUNDS at 5.24% as of May-June 2012
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of cases, or the interaction term (inflation × FEDFUNDS) shows Rank IC < 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2.0% annualized signals sub-trend economic expansion and rising recession risk. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity investors reprice terminal value and growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11443.5 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2012
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession warning
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters with GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC with GDPC1 growth rate falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,500 thousand units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously grow above 2% month-over-month. This joint demand expansion signals strong consumer confidence and forward economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1588.0 and RSAFS at 369865.0 as of May 2012 show demand strength
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with robust consumer and housing demand
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in more than 62% of cases, or the interaction term (HOUST level × RSAFS growth) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
